tools.name: getPromptMeta
tools.description: Retrieves orchestration metadata for a Crafted Intelligence companion prompt. Returns the extension record data: domain, toolset, tool chain, steps (JSON), edition, params (JSON), safety rules (JSON), governance level, and artifact hints. The AI calls this as its FIRST step when executing any Crafted prompt — it provides the full context needed to invoke the correct tools in the correct order. Pass either the Atlas prompt internal ID or the Crafted external ID (aiprompt_crafted_*). Returns the prompt text along with all orchestration metadata. JSON fields (steps, params, safety_rules, tool_deps) are parsed into structured objects. If no extension record exists for the given prompt, returns an error — only Crafted prompts with extension records are supported.

promptId.description: Internal ID of the Atlas AI Companion prompt record. Example: 101 for Barrel Profile Lookup. Use this when you know the prompt's internal ID.

externalId.description: External ID of the Atlas prompt (e.g., 'aiprompt_crafted_barrel_001'). Used if promptId is not provided. Matches the externalid field on customrecord_atlas_aicomp_prompts.

meta_id.description: Internal ID of the extension record (customrecord_dz_prompt_meta).

prompt_id.description: Internal ID of the Atlas companion prompt.

prompt_text.description: Full prompt text from Atlas (clean, user-facing).

domain.description: Crafted domain: Barrel Operations, Lot Profitability, Inventory & Supply Chain, Compliance & Audit, MRP Intelligence, or Batch & Genealogy.

steps.description: Parsed JSON array of orchestration steps with call, purpose, condition, depends_on, params_from.

tool_deps.description: Parsed JSON array of required toolset script IDs.

edition.description: Target edition: Distillery, Winery, Brewery, or Cross-Edition.

params.description: Parsed JSON parameter schema with types, hints, resolves_to.

safety_rules.description: Parsed JSON array of non-hallucination and data quality rules.

governance.description: Governance tier: Minimal, Standard, Governed, or Supervised.

artifact.description: Whether this prompt generates a visual artifact.

version.description: Semantic version of the extension record.

status.description: Lifecycle status: Active, Draft, Deprecated, or Testing.

tools.name: logExecution
tools.description: Creates a lightweight execution log record (customrecord_dz_exec_log) for a companion prompt run. Records which prompt was executed, which tools were called, whether it succeeded or failed, duration in seconds, which prompt version was running, and which AI agent ran it. Called by the AI after completing (or failing) a prompt execution. Returns the log record ID on success.

promptId.description: Internal ID of the Atlas prompt that was executed.

toolsCalled.description: JSON array string of tool names called during execution. Example: '["getBarrelProfile","getBarrelCostTrace"]'.

duration.description: Total execution duration in seconds from first tool call to final output.

error.description: Error message or failure reason if execution failed. Omit on success.

promptVersion.description: Semantic version of the prompt extension record at execution time (e.g., '1.0.0'). Copy from getPromptMeta response.

agent.description: AI agent name that executed the prompt. Valid values: 'claude', 'chatgpt'.

success.description: Whether the log record was created.

logId.description: Internal ID of the created execution log record.

tools.name: seedPrompt
tools.description: Creates an Atlas AI Companion prompt and its Crafted extension record in a single idempotent operation. If a prompt with the given externalId already exists, returns the existing record IDs without creating a duplicate. Use to programmatically seed domain prompts into the Companion Library. Creates the Atlas prompt with category=Manufacturing, role=Administrator, industry=Food & Beverage defaults. Pass extension record fields (domain, toolset, steps, etc.) to create the orchestration metadata alongside the prompt.

externalId.description: Unique external ID for idempotent creation (e.g., 'aiprompt_crafted_barrel_003'). Must start with 'aiprompt_crafted_'.

promptText.description: Clean, user-facing prompt text. No orchestration metadata — that goes in extension record fields.

domain.description: Extension record domain list value ID. Values: 1=Barrel Operations, 2=Lot Profitability, 3=Inventory & Supply Chain, 4=Compliance & Audit, 5=MRP Intelligence, 6=Batch & Genealogy.

subdomain.description: Finer categorization (e.g., 'Barrel Valuation & Finance'). Also sets Atlas prompt subcategory.

steps.description: JSON string of orchestration steps array.

toolDeps.description: JSON string array of required toolset script IDs.

safetyRules.description: JSON string array of safety rules.

governance.description: Governance level list value ID. Values: 1=Minimal, 2=Standard, 3=Governed, 4=Supervised.

artifact.description: Whether prompt generates a visual artifact.

action.description: 'created' (new prompt + extension), 'extended' (existing prompt, new extension), or 'existing' (both already exist).

promptId.description: Internal ID of the Atlas prompt.

metaId.description: Internal ID of the extension record.

tools.name: updatePrompt
tools.description: Updates an existing Atlas companion prompt text and/or its Crafted extension record fields. Automatically bumps the patch version on the extension record (e.g., 1.0.0 -> 1.0.1) unless a specific newVersion is provided. Only provided fields are updated — omitted fields are left unchanged. Can update Atlas-level fields (promptText, promptName) and extension record fields (domain, toolset, steps, safety rules, etc.) in a single call.

promptId.description: Internal ID of the Atlas prompt to update.

promptText.description: New prompt text. If omitted, prompt text is not changed.

promptName.description: New prompt display name. If omitted, name is not changed.

subdomain.description: New subdomain. Updates both extension record and Atlas subcategory.

governance.description: New governance level list value ID (1-4).

newVersion.description: Explicit new version (e.g., '2.0.0'). If omitted, patch version is auto-incremented.

updatedFields.description: Array of field names that were updated.
